$comment: evolve: apply ONE spec layer to an already-built module as a TRANSACTIONAL cross-cutting edit. A change layer is NOT append-only growth (that is compose/add_unit) - it changes existing units and their callers TOGETHER, so it must regenerate all changed units at once and verify the whole module ONCE, rolling back on failure (the coedit/stage_files pattern). Steps: sync_layer checks out spec/<to> into specs/; evolve_ctx assembles the changed set (each unit's NEW spec + its CURRENT file, derived from the spec_diff between spec/<from> and spec/<to>); module_api gives the exact surface of the unchanged units; the model regenerates EVERY changed/added file as one marker payload; stage_files writes them all and verifies go vet + go test -race over the whole module, rolling back everything if it fails; one bounded repair. Input: "<from> <to>" (e.g. "L0 L1"); --ws <proj>. Bootstrap L0 first: `/do sync_layer <proj> L0` then `/flow compose --ws <proj>`.
summary: Apply one spec layer as a transactional multi-file edit: regenerate every added/changed unit together, verify the whole module once (go vet + go test -race), roll back on failure.
